questions.question: Explain the architecture of Ethereum blockchain, including its main components and how they interact.
keyPoints: Consensus layer using Proof of Stake
State management with Merkle Patricia Tries
questions.modelAnswer: Ethereum is a decentralized blockchain platform with a layered architecture. The Network Layer handles P2P communication using devp2p protocol. The Consensus Layer implements Proof of Stake where validators stake 32 ETH to participate in block validation. The Execution Layer runs the EVM for smart contract execution. State is maintained using Merkle Patricia Tries for efficient verification. Blocks contain headers with state roots, transaction roots, and receipts roots. Two account types exist: Externally Owned Accounts (EOAs) controlled by private keys and Contract Accounts containing bytecode.

keyPoints: Combination of Merkle tree and Patricia trie
State trie, storage trie, transaction trie
